# Native SDK soundboard example

A music-library browser built to showcase native-rendered Native SDK UI: an album grid with real cover art through the runtime image pipeline, track lists with native context menus, a now-playing bar with real audio playback, transport controls and a live seek slider, and search with the field's built-in clear affordance. The custom light/dark theme follows the system appearance live — there is no in-window theme control.

**The catalog is real data.** `src/music_manifest.zon` is a committed manifest generated by `tools/prepare-example-music.sh`; the model imports it as a typed value and flattens the albums and tracks at comptime. Albums carry a variable number of tracks, so each album records where its run starts and how long it is — nothing assumes a fixed stride.

**Playback is real audio.** Pressing play issues `fx.playAudio` for the track's file through the runtime audio effect family; the load acknowledgment, coarse position ticks, the one completion at natural end, and failures all arrive as typed messages through the ordinary update path. Positions are the player's real clock; the player's own duration reports are estimates for this catalog (the prepare script strips the seek header, so a decoder can only extrapolate from bitrate — a mid-stream guess can be minutes off) and never replace the manifest's measured duration, so the transport's total always matches the track list. The scrubber moves every frame, not every tick: while playing, `on_frame` feeds the model a frame clock that advances the rendered position between the ~500 ms position ticks (ticks correct it, never rewinding it mid-motion), and the loop starves itself the moment playback stops — pause, and zero frames present. The audio files themselves are gitignored, but nothing needs preparing to try this: the committed manifest carries a hosted `.url_base`, so a fresh clone streams every track on demand (next paragraph). `tools/prepare-example-music.sh` is the path for offline local files and for regenerating or self-hosting the catalog: it prepares the mp3s under `assets/music/` from a source library and rewrites the manifest (local files always win over the stream). With streaming explicitly disabled (`NATIVE_SDK_MUSIC_URL_BASE` set empty) and no local files, a play attempt degrades honestly: the now-playing bar shows "music assets not prepared — run tools/prepare-example-music.sh", browsing keeps working fully, and the deterministic test suite passes with no audio files present.

**Tracks stream on demand out of the box.** The committed manifest's `.url_base` points at the hosted mirror of the prepared catalog — the exact bytes the prepare script writes — and `NATIVE_SDK_MUSIC_URL_BASE` overrides it at launch (a locally served pack works: `python3 -m http.server` over `assets/music/`'s parent; set it empty to disable streaming entirely). A missing local file streams the track instead of failing: playback starts while the bytes are still arriving (the artist line reads "buffering…" while the stream stalls — an honest state distinct from playing), and the same bytes fill a local cache so the next play of that track is local and instant. The cache lives at `~/Library/Caches/soundboard/audio/` (the platform caches convention), keyed by URL hash and size-verified against the manifest's per-track `.bytes` — a partial or stale entry never plays; delete that directory to clear the cache. Offline with a cold cache, the bar says "stream unavailable — check the connection and try again" instead of blaming the prepare script.

## Authoring split (markup-first, two shells)

One codebase carries two shells: the content pieces are shared functions and fragments, and the root view switches the composition on the model's form factor: the HOST-REPORTED size class when one arrived over the window-chrome channel (the iOS host reports its horizontal size class there), with the width derivation as the fallback (compact strictly below the desktop window's 1056pt min content width — the floor the desktop layout audit proves clean). The host report landed inside `Model.formFactor` without reshaping any view, exactly as designed.

- **Shared content** (`src/view.zig`) — the album tile, the adaptive grid body and its width→columns rule (each shell states its own tile minimum), the track row (content shared, gestures per shell), the album detail cover and heading block, section headings, the empty state, and the compiled `album_title.native` fragment. The now-playing metadata (`nowPlayingTitle`/`nowPlayingArtist`/`nowPlayingCover`/`playPauseIcon`) is shared at the model level: the desktop markup bar and the compact mini bar bind the same functions, so the status surface (assets notice, stream notice, buffering) reads identically in both shells.
- **Desktop shell** — `src/header.native` (the flush `<button-group>` switcher, the search field, the window-drag titlebar band padded past the OS window controls via `on_chrome`) and `src/nowplaying.native` (circular cover, title/artist, prev/play/next, seek slider, time labels, queue badge), composed around the shared content. Desktop gestures: click selects, double-click/Enter plays, right/ctrl-click opens the native context menus.
- **Compact shell** (`src/view.zig`, Zig — shell branching is Zig's job; the closed markup grammar has no conditionals) — a single-column phone composition: a stacked touch header (the same switcher and search binding at lg control size, search on its own full-width row), the albums grid at a 150pt tile floor (two columns on mainstream phone widths), the album detail as a full-surface page with model-driven back navigation, and a now-playing MINI BAR (cover thumb, title/artist, play/pause) that stays visible whenever a track is loaded or a playback notice needs a surface. Touch gestures: one tap plays a row; no hover affordances, no double-tap, no context menus (the phone hosts model no long-press), no window-drag regions. The shell pads all four safe-area edges from the window-chrome channel (`on_chrome` — the iOS host republishes the safe areas there), so content clears the Dynamic Island and the home indicator.
- `src/model.zig` — the typed manifest import and its comptime flattening, playback/search/queue/appearance state, the controlled scroll offsets (each region's `on_scroll` stores the applied offset and the view echoes it back, so a rebuild mid-gesture never resets a list), the form-factor rule, and `update`. Both shells read one model and one Msg set, so the same Msg journal replays deterministically into either shell (pinned by a test).
- `src/theme.zig` — the Geist pack with the accent moved to the pack's pink scale (one brand decision layered through `DesignTokenOverrides`); high-contrast requests take the pack's own loud register untouched and reduce-motion zeroes the motion tokens.

`src/main.zig` carries both entries: `main` runs the desktop scene (window constants, tall hidden-inset titlebar), and `initModel`/`mobileOptions` compile the same app into the mobile embed library (`native dev --target ios`) on the canonical full-screen mobile scene — no desktop window constant constrains the phone, and the mobile boot model seeds a phone-portrait canvas width so the installing frame already composes the compact shell.

All `.native` files compile at comptime (`canvas.CompiledMarkupView`); the test suite additionally builds the header and now-playing bars through the runtime interpreter and asserts engine parity.

## Cover art

The committed covers in `src/art/` are 512px JPEGs, one per album, named by the manifest's `art` slots and embedded at comptime. Live macOS decodes them through the platform codec seam; the null platform's strict test decoder speaks only the deterministic PNG subset, so under `-Dplatform=null` registration fails and every album degrades to its initials fallback — the honest codec-less-host state the suite pins. `init_fx` registers the covers at boot; a failed decode leaves that album on initials, never a broken boot.

## Fixed capacities

- The manifest's albums and tracks, flattened at comptime (variable tracks per album).
- One registered cover image per album, of the runtime's 16 image slots.
- 16-entry play-next queue; a full queue drops the request and counts it.
- 48-byte search buffer.
- Two context-menu items per track row: the all-songs list mounts every catalog track as a row, comfortably inside the 512-item per-view budget.
- The widget tree peaks in the all-songs list (one row per catalog track), held under 768 nodes of the 1024 per-view budget by a test.

## Run

```sh
native dev
```

That is the whole setup: tracks stream from the hosted catalog and cache locally on first play. To play from local files instead (offline, or your own catalog), run `tools/prepare-example-music.sh` from the repo root — it prepares the gitignored mp3s under `assets/music/` from a source library, and local files always win over the stream.

Right/ctrl-click a track row for the native context menu; Copy Title pipes the title through `pbcopy`. The theme follows the system appearance live — flip the OS to dark and the studio palette follows.

Track rows follow the desktop list convention: a single click SELECTS a row (the accent-filled selection), a double click plays it. `Up`/`Down` move the selection — never playback, and never the focus ring (outlines belong to Tab) — and `Enter` plays the selected track. `Space` toggles play/pause from anywhere — the media-app convention, carried by the app-level key fallback (`on_key`). A RING-focused widget wins first: space on a tabbed-to track row selects it, Enter plays it, a focused header segment switches, and a focused search field keeps typing spaces (the text-entry exception is structural, by widget kind); a quietly focused row — the state a click leaves behind — stays transparent, so clicking around the library never re-aims the spacebar.

Run the deterministic suite (dispatch and playback through the fake effects executor, the cover decode/register/draw path, theming, markup engine parity, layout budgets — hermetic, no audio files needed):

```sh
native test -Dplatform=null
```

Verify live through the automation harness:

```sh
native build -Dautomation=true
./zig-out/bin/soundboard &
native automate assert 'gpu_nonblank=true' 'role=listitem name="Exit Signs by Harbor Sleep"' 'role=button name="Play or pause"'
```
